biodiversity_commitments.text: achieving a net positive impact on biodiversity by 2030
The Plan also endorses the principle of no net deforestation, promoting it among key suppliers through awareness-raising initiatives.
Number of facilities aligned with a biodiversity plan: 2025: 199, 2028e: >450, 2030e: >700

climate_targets.text: reduce the emissions intensity of electricity generation to 32 g CO2/kWh in a normal rainfall period by 2028
achieve CO2eq neutrality for Scope 1 by 2030, with a target figure of less than 10 g CO2/kWh in intensity from electricity generation emissions that year
reduce intensity of specific CO2 emissions to 70g CO2/kWh by 2025
Comply SBTi milestone in 2030 & 2040 for Net Zero in scopes 1, 2 and 3 before 2040
Increase Managed Stored Energy Capacity to >11,000 GWh/year by 2030
Achieve ~90% asset automation for Smart Grids by 2028
Accrue >30,000 Million Euros (cumulative) of Green Financing by 2028

water_disclosures.text: Reduce water intensity in energy production by 20% by 2028 and by 40% by 2030 (vs base year).
Total water consumption - Continuing activities: 45,642,187 m3 in 2025.
Total water consumption in water stress areas - Continuing activities: 41,912,946 m3 in 2025.
Amount of water recycled/reused - Continuing activities: 8,466,671,184 m3 in 2025.
100 % Of total water withdrawn is used in cooling processes.

supply_chain_claims.text: social and sustainability audits were conducted at ten manufacturing sites in China belonging to five major global photovoltaic module manufacturers in 2025
achieve >50,000 Million Euros (cumulative) awarded to sustainable suppliers by 2028

The volume of general supplies invoiced to the Iberdrola Group by suppliers with a documented or certified environmental management system accounted for 83.9% of the total volume billed. In the case of fuel suppliers, those with a certified environmental management system accounted for 89.5% of those assessed.
A total of 42 audits were conducted on key suppliers in the Group’s main geographical areas, confirming good on-site practices and progress on action plans with minimal deviations.

top_sustainability_claims.text: Meeting all challenges: we’re building a solid, secure and robust network of opportunities that lead to the future. A pathway of opportunities driven by electrification, technology, digitalisation, productivity and operational efficiency, extending to all the stakeholders that drive our ecosystem: shareholders who trust in our vision, employees who develop high-impact solutions, and a society that benefits from industrial progress.
Iberdrola has announced its ambition to achieve Net Zero emissions by 2040.
Iberdrola defined its Biodiversity Plan 2030 with the central objective of achieving a net positive impact on biodiversity by 2030.

Strong and continuous reduction in the intensity of direct emissions, from 250 g/kWh in 2010, to 39 gCO2/kWh in 2025, a reduction of 84%. Since 2020, a 60% reduction in emissions intensity has been achieved.

The Board of Directors stands out for maintaining a gender balance, with 50% external directors of each gender and no gender with a representation of less than 40% of all directors (43% women and 57% men).

document_summary: Iberdrola's 2025 sustainability report details its performance across environmental, social, and governance areas, emphasizing its commitment to achieving Net Zero emissions by 2040 and a net positive impact on biodiversity by 2030, with SBTi-validated targets. The report highlights efforts in climate action, water management, supply chain sustainability, and workforce diversity. It also details the company's governance structure and stakeholder engagement processes.
extraction_notes: The document is comprehensive and well-structured, providing clear statements and quantitative data for most requested fields. It has a strong focus on climate and biodiversity, with detailed tables and metrics. Information on forest risk commodities was limited to a general mention of 'wood'.
